debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
x y : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n
f : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n → ℝ
⊢ deriv (GlobalTheorem.interpolated_deriv✝ n x y f) = GlobalTheorem.interpolated_deriv2✝ n x y f

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h1 : deriv (interpolated_deriv x y f) = interpolated_deriv2 x y f := by
    -- Prove by simplifying the expression and using known calculus results.
    simp [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, Function.comp_apply, mul_comm]
    <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try simp_all [Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_sub_distrib, Finset.sum_add_distrib]) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try field_simp) <;>
    (try linarith)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        -- Use the fact that the second derivative is symmetric and can be simplified accordingly.
        have h2 : ∀ (i j : Fin n), (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i <| nth_partial j f) ((1 - (0 : ℝ)) • x + (0 : ℝ) • y) = (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i <| nth_partial j f) x := by
          intro i j
          simp [add_smul, sub_smul, one_smul]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp_all
          <;> linarith
        simp_all [h2]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp_all
        <;> linarith
      }
    )
  exact h1
